chief_complaint: A 30-year-old man presents with a history of perinatally acquired HIV, complicated by incomplete adherence, multidrug-resistant HIV, and opportunistic infections.
history: The patient has a history of perinatally acquired HIV, complicated by incomplete adherence, multidrug-resistant HIV, and opportunistic infections. He was initially treated with antifungal and antimycobacterials with oral antiretroviral therapy (ART), but persistent high HIV viral load prompted initiation of long-acting injectable ART (LAI-ART) with cabotegravir and rilpivirine combined with adjunct oral nucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitor therapy (emtricitabine/tenofovir).
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination findings: not provided.
labs: HIV viral load: >100,000 copies/mL. CD4 cell count: 209 cells/microL/18.7%. Resistance testing: high-level resistance to emtricitabine and intermediate resistance to rilpivirine.
